postflopScenarios.title: Dry A-high rainbow
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop A♠8♦3♣. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Dry A-high rainbow: massive range advantage. BB 3-bets most strong Ax preflop. Small c-bet (~33%) at high frequency because calling ranges are inelastic on dry textures. KQs has overcards and backdoor flush.

postflopScenarios.title: Wet connected board
postflopScenarios.situation: CO opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop 9♥8♥7♠. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: 987 connects heavily with BB: straights, two-pair, sets, combo draws. AK has zero equity and no draw. Solvers check ~100%. Betting folds nothing worse and gets called/raised by everything better.

postflopScenarios.title: K-high dry board
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop K♠7♦2♣. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: K72 rainbow is very dry. You have range advantage with all overpairs and Kx. Small c-bet pressures BB's wide range. AJ has decent equity with overcard + gutshot to Broadway.

postflopScenarios.title: Monotone flop
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop T♣6♣2♣. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: On monotone boards, BB has more flushes (wider preflop range includes low suited clubs). Your QJdd has zero club equity. Solvers check this hand. Betting risks getting check-raised by made flushes and draws you cannot beat.

postflopScenarios.title: Paired board small bet
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop 8♠8♦3♥. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Paired boards: neither player likely has an 8. Board is effectively dry. Solvers use high-frequency tiny bet (~25%) with entire range. AK has massive range advantage. Small bet pressures BB at minimal risk.

postflopScenarios.title: Low connected board
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop 7♥6♠5♦. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: On 765, BB has many straights and draws, but AA is a strong overpair that needs protection. A larger bet (~75%) charges draws and denies equity. Checking risks giving free cards to hands with 40%+ equity against you.

postflopScenarios.title: Q-high wet board
postflopScenarios.situation: CO opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop Q♠T♦4♣. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: QT4 is semi-wet with Broadway draws. You have a gutshot + two overcards. A ~66% bet works because fold equity is valuable (getting Ax/low pairs to fold) and you can improve to a straight on A/9.

postflopScenarios.title: A-K-x nut advantage
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop A♠K♦2♣. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: AK2r is the strongest BTN flop. BB 3-bets AA/KK/AK preflop, so you hold hands they can never beat. Textbook nut advantage = overbet. Build the pot aggressively.

postflopScenarios.title: J-high dry flop
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop J♠5♦2♥. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: J52 rainbow is dry. You have an open-ended straight draw (8 and Q give you a straight). Small c-bet is good for two reasons: you can take it down now, and if called you have a strong draw to continue with on the turn.

postflopScenarios.title: OOP c-bet decision
postflopScenarios.situation: You 3-bet from SB, BU calls. 30BB effective. Flop K♣J♠4♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: As the 3-bettor OOP, you have a range advantage on KJ4. Small c-bet (~33%) at high frequency is standard. AQ has a gutshot to Broadway (T gives you a straight). Checking would give up initiative with a hand that has good equity.

postflopScenarios.title: BB check-raise flush draw
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. 40BB eff. Flop T♠6♠2♦. BU bets 33%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: 75ss has flush draw (9 outs) + gutshot (4 outs) = 13 outs. Premium check-raise candidate. Check-raising wins the pot immediately or gives you strong equity if called. 3x is standard sizing.

postflopScenarios.title: BB check-raise two pair
postflopScenarios.situation: CO opens, you call BB. 35BB eff. Flop 8♠6♦2♣. CO bets 33%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Two pair on a dry board is a strong value check-raise. CO will c-bet many overcards and overpairs. You want to build the pot now before the board gets scary. 3x sizing is standard and gives you a good SPR for turn play.

postflopScenarios.title: BB check-raise as bluff
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. 40BB eff. Flop K♠9♦5♣. BU bets 33%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: A4cc has a backdoor flush draw + gutshot (need a 2/3 to make a straight). This is a good bluff check-raise candidate because: you have some equity if called, you fold out unpaired hands, and you balance your value check-raises.

postflopScenarios.title: Facing c-bet with top pair
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. 40BB eff. Flop K♠7♦3♣. BU bets 33%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: K9 is top pair with a medium kicker. It is strong enough to continue but not strong enough to check-raise for value (you only get action from better Kx and sets). Call and re-evaluate the turn. Save your check-raises for two-pair+ and strong draws.

postflopScenarios.title: Set on wet board
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. 40BB eff. Flop 9♥7♣6♠. BU bets 50%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Set of 6s on a connected board. You MUST check-raise for value here. The board is dangerous: 85, T8, 58 all make straights. Building the pot now maximizes value before scare cards come. Many draws and overpairs will call.

postflopScenarios.title: Continue on safe turn
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You c-bet 33% on A♣8♦3♠, BB calls. Turn 2♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: You have top pair top kicker on a brick turn. BB's calling range on the flop includes medium Ax, pocket pairs, and some draws. A ~66% turn bet targets the medium Ax hands (A9-AJ) and charges flush draws. This is a clear value bet.

postflopScenarios.title: Give up on scary turn
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You c-bet 33% on J♦7♣2♥, BB calls. Turn 7♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Turn 7 pairs the board, giving BB all trips combos (87, 97, T7, A7, etc.). Your KQ has only overcards. BB's calling range on J72 includes many 7x hands. Checking is clearly best. You have showdown value with K-high but no reason to keep bluffing into trips.

postflopScenarios.title: Bluff barrel with draw
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You c-bet 50% on J♣5♥2♥, BB calls. Turn 4♣.
postflopScenarios.explanation: You have a flush draw that missed the turn but still has 9 outs. The 4 is a brick for BB. Barreling ~75% continues to apply pressure. You have great equity if called (~35% to make the flush) and fold out some pairs.

postflopScenarios.title: Overbet turn nut advantage
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You c-bet 50% on K♠Q♦3♣, BB calls. Turn 3♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Turn 3 pairs the board, giving you the only full house (AA33). BB cannot have a full house (would have 3-bet KK/QQ). You now have massive nut advantage. Overbet extracts maximum value from Kx and Qx that must call.

postflopScenarios.title: Check back medium pair
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You c-bet 33% on Q♣8♦4♠, BB calls. Turn J♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: TT is an underpair that just got worse. Turn J gives BB more Jx hands that beat you. You have some showdown value but should not bet for protection or value. Checking preserves your equity and avoids getting check-raised off your hand.

postflopScenarios.title: Thin value with top pair
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. Bet flop and turn, both called. River 6♣.
postflopScenarios.explanation: A9 on A7426 is top pair with a medium kicker. After two streets of action, BB has medium Ax (A8-A5), pocket pairs, and maybe some missed draws. A small value bet (~33%) targets the weaker Ax hands. Larger sizes only get called by better.

postflopScenarios.title: Set on brick river
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. Bet flop and turn, both called. River 2♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Set of Jacks on a brick river. BB called two streets, so their range includes Jx, Tx, and stubborn pairs. ~75% pot targets Tx and Jx. Overbet risks folding out the medium hands. 33% leaves money on the table.

postflopScenarios.title: Rivered flush value
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop with draw, checked turn. River 3♥ completes your flush.
postflopScenarios.explanation: You rivered the second nut flush. Since you checked the turn, your range is perceived as weaker. A ~75% bet can get called by Jx, 9x, and even smaller flushes that think you might be bluffing.

postflopScenarios.title: Value bet vs capped range
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop, BB calls. Turn check-check. River 9♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: QJ is top pair good kicker. After BB just called flop and both checked turn, BB's range is capped (no monsters). A ~50% bet extracts value from 8x, 9x, and lower Qx. Larger bets only get called by hands that beat you.

postflopScenarios.title: Two pair on paired board
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop 50%, BB calls. Turn T♠ check-check. River 4♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: K8 makes two pair (kings and eights) on the river, but the board now has a pair of 4s. You need to value bet because BB can have Kx, Tx, 8x. ~50% pot is right sizing to get calls from these hands.

postflopScenarios.title: Missed flush draw bluff
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop, bet turn, both called. River 7♣ misses your flush.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Your flush draw bricked but you have a natural bluff. You repped strength on two streets and A♠ blocks the nut flush draw BB might have called with. An overbet puts maximum pressure on BB's bluff catchers (Kx, Jx).

postflopScenarios.title: Blocker-based river bluff
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. C-bet flop, barrel turn, BB calls both. River 8♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: AT has zero showdown value but also zero blockers to BB's strong hands. BB called two streets with likely Kx, QK, sets. Your A blocks AK but not KQ, KJ. Without good blockers, a third barrel is too risky. Checking and giving up is the disciplined play.

postflopScenarios.title: Block bet as bluff
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop draw, checked turn. River A♠.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Your draw missed but the A is a great bluff card. BB's range after calling flop and checking turn has many Qx/Tx hands that now face the scary Ace. A tiny blocking bet (~25%) can fold out Qx/Tx cheaply without risking much.

postflopScenarios.title: Double barrel bluff turn+river
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. C-bet flop with flush draw, barrel turn on K. River T♦ misses.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Flush draw bricked but you barreled AK. T is another overcard that doesn't help BB's medium pairs. A ~75% river bet tells a credible story of AK, KK, or AA. BB must fold most Ax and pocket pairs. Your 9-high has no showdown value anyway.

postflopScenarios.title: Bluff when draw completes
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop, turn check-check. River 8♥ completes 96 straight.
postflopScenarios.explanation: The 8 completes a possible straight (96, J9) and makes the board scarier. You have a gutshot that missed but QJ blocks QT and JT which are strong hands BB might hold. Betting ~75% represents the straight and pressures one-pair hands.

postflopScenarios.title: Top pair vs river bet
postflopScenarios.situation: CO opens, you call BB. CO bets all three streets, river bet is 75% pot.
postflopScenarios.explanation: MDF vs 75% pot = Pot/(Pot+Bet) = ~57%. You must defend ~57% of your range. KT is top pair with a decent kicker, beating all bluffs (missed draws, AJ, AT). Folding makes your range exploitable. This is a textbook bluff catcher. Call.

postflopScenarios.title: Underpair vs overbet
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. BU bets flop and turn, then overbets 150% pot on river.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Against an overbet (150% pot), MDF = ~40%. You only need to defend with 40% of your range. 99 is only 4th pair on KT5. Against an overbet range that is very polarized (strong value or pure bluffs), 99 is near the bottom of your range. Fold.

postflopScenarios.title: Second pair facing small bet
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. BU checks flop, bets turn 50%, river 33%.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Second pair (tens) against a small river bet is a clear call. The small sizing indicates BU is either thin value betting or trying to get a cheap bluff through. QT with a queen kicker beats most of BU's thin value range.

postflopScenarios.title: Ace-high on scary board
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. BU c-bets flop, checks turn, bets 66% river.
postflopScenarios.explanation: AJ is ace-high, no pair. Board has K and Q, and BU bet-check-bet. This line often means BU has a medium made hand that checked turn for pot control. AJ only beats pure bluffs. Against this line, your ace-high is too weak. Fold.

postflopScenarios.title: Mid pair vs delayed barrel
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. BU checks flop, checks turn, bets 75% river.
postflopScenarios.explanation: BU checked two streets then bet river. This is a polarized line: either a missed draw turning into a bluff or a slowplayed monster. 88 beats all bluffs. On this board, BU can have many missed hands (KQ, KJ, QJ). Call.

postflopScenarios.title: Choose between small and large
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 40BB, BB calls. Flop K♣7♠2♦. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: AA on K72 rainbow. You have an overpair on a dry board. Normally dry boards call for small bets, but here you have a specific hand (not a range). AA wants protection from Kx hands and wants to build a pot. ~66% targets Kx that will call.

postflopScenarios.title: Geometric sizing planning
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens 30BB eff, BB calls. Flop Q♠8♦3♥. BB checks. You plan to bet all three streets.
postflopScenarios.explanation: With 30BB effective and 5bb pot, geometric sizing means choosing a size that gets you all-in by the river. ~33% pot on flop, ~50% turn, ~66% river achieves this. Starting with 33% keeps BB's range wide for future streets.

postflopScenarios.title: Small bet OOP 3-bet pot
postflopScenarios.situation: You 3-bet from BB, BU calls. 40BB eff. Flop J♠8♣4♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: In 3-bet pots OOP, solvers favor small c-bets (~25%) at high frequency. AKcc has overcards + backdoor flush. Small sizing keeps your range together (you bet with your entire range for this price) and does not overcommit to the pot.

postflopScenarios.title: Overbet river with nuts
postflopScenarios.situation: BU vs BB. You bet flop and turn, called both. River K♥.
postflopScenarios.explanation: Set of queens with a K on the river. K♥ is an excellent card because it gives BB many Kx hands they will never fold. Your hand is the nuts (only QQ or full houses beat you, which BB does not have). Overbet maximizes value.

postflopScenarios.title: Donk bet on dynamic turn
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. BU checks flop 8♣7♥3♦. Turn 6♠ gives you a straight.
postflopScenarios.explanation: You just made the nut straight. BU checked the flop, showing weakness. If you check, BU might check again. A ~75% donk bet looks suspicious but gets value from BU's overpairs and draws that won't fold. You need to build the pot now.

postflopScenarios.title: Three-way pot caution
postflopScenarios.situation: You open CO, BU and BB both call. 3-way. Flop A♣T♠6♥. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: In multiway pots, ranges are stronger because multiple players saw the flop. However, AK is the near-nuts here. Bet ~66% because you need to charge draws and worse Ax. In multiway, bet with a tighter/stronger range but don't slow play top pair top kicker.

postflopScenarios.title: Multiway overpair check
postflopScenarios.situation: You open HJ, CO and BB both call. 3-way. Flop Q♦8♣5♠. BB checks.
postflopScenarios.explanation: JJ is an underpair in a 3-way pot on a Q-high board. In multiway pots, solvers check much more because there are two opponents who could have Qx. JJ has showdown value. Check and reevaluate. Don't bloat the pot with a hand that is often behind.

postflopScenarios.title: Probe bet on favorable turn
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. Flop Q♠7♦3♥, BU checks. Turn K♠.
postflopScenarios.explanation: BU checked the flop, showing weakness. Turn K improves your hand to top pair. This is a textbook probe spot. A ~66% bet attacks BU's capped range (would have bet the flop with strong Qx). Your K9 is now the best hand most of the time.

postflopScenarios.title: Don't probe with weak hand
postflopScenarios.situation: CO opens, you call BB. Flop A♣K♠5♥, CO checks. Turn J♦.
postflopScenarios.explanation: 87dd has no pair and only a backdoor flush draw on AKJ. CO checked the flop but AK5 is a board where CO can trap with strong hands. Probing with pure air on this texture is too risky. Check and take the free card.

postflopScenarios.title: Probe with gutshot
postflopScenarios.situation: BU opens, you call BB. Flop Q♦5♣2♥, BU checks. Turn 8♣.
postflopScenarios.explanation: T9 picked up a gutshot (J gives a straight) and has overcards. BU showed weakness by checking the flop. A ~50% probe bet attacks their capped range and gives you fold equity plus draw equity if called.

tips.text: Ranges widen toward the button and as stacks shrink.
At 15BB or less, many hands become jam-only. Don't raise-fold short.
Range advantage determines bet FREQUENCY. Nut advantage determines bet SIZE.
Dry boards: ~33% c-bet high freq. Wet boards: check more often.
MDF: vs 50% pot defend 67%. vs 75% defend 57%. vs pot defend 50%.
Variance is math. Even winning players face 10-15 BI downswings.
Decisions over results. A correct losing call is still correct.
MTTs: 100-200 BIs. Cash: 30-50 BIs. Max 5% per event.
Study BU vs BB first. Most common and highest-volume spot.
Opponents fold >60% to c-bets: bluff more. Call >50%: value bet wider.
